summary: Iteration 6 testing complete - Wake word activation feature fully tested. All backend API tests pass (9/9). Frontend UI elements verified: wake word toggle, 'Ativacao por Voz' section, dynamic 'Hey {agent_name}' text, terminal info box when enabled, wake word indicator element. Web Speech API features can't be tested in headless browser but UI components are correctly implemented.
critical_code_review_comments: SettingsUpdate model correctly includes wake_word_enabled: Optional[bool] field
PUT /api/settings correctly persists wake_word_enabled to database
GET /api/settings returns wake_word_enabled field (may be None for existing users without the field)
WakeWordListener component uses Web Speech API with continuous=true for passive listening
Wake phrase triggers: 'hey {name}', 'ei {name}', 'oi {name}', 'hei {name}' - supports Portuguese variations
WakeWordListener only renders when enabled AND hands-free mode is NOT showing (prevents conflict)
Wake word indicator (data-testid='wake-word-indicator') positioned fixed bottom-left with status indicator
Settings panel 'Voz e Audio' section shows both TTS toggle and wake word toggle
Dynamic wake phrase text updates when agent_name changes in settings
seed_data_creation: No new seed data - using existing admin user
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 added wake word activation feature. Key test points: 1) PUT /api/settings with wake_word_enabled=true/false persists correctly. 2) GET /api/settings returns wake_word_enabled field. 3) Settings panel has 'Ativacao por Voz' toggle (data-testid='wake-word-toggle'). 4) Dynamic 'Hey {agent_name}' text updates when agent name changes. 5) Terminal info box appears when wake word enabled showing 'Escuta passiva ativa...'. 6) Wake word indicator (data-testid='wake-word-indicator') appears in bottom-left when enabled. 7) WakeWordListener uses Web Speech API - won't work in headless browser but UI verified. 8) Previous features (hands-free mode, TTS, agent personality) still working.

wake_word_ui.Wake word indicator shows status: PASS - Shows 'Off' (Web Speech API not available in headless)

notes.web_speech_api: WakeWordListener uses browser Web Speech API with continuous=true for passive listening. This won't work in headless browser testing but all UI elements were verified.
notes.wake_phrases: Supports multiple trigger phrases: 'hey {name}', 'ei {name}', 'oi {name}', 'hei {name}' - accommodates Portuguese pronunciation variations
notes.indicator_status: Wake word indicator shows 'Off' in headless browser because Web Speech API can't start without user interaction. In real browser with microphone permission, it would show 'listening' status.
notes.component_logic: WakeWordListener only renders when: 1) wake_word_enabled is true, AND 2) hands-free mode is NOT showing (to prevent conflicts)
